what: Regression Calibration for Measurement Error Correction
title: Regression Calibration for Measurement Error Correction
text: RegCalib R package correcting measurement error continuous exposures (covariates) using regression calibration. provides corrected coefficients, standard errors, p-values, variance-covariance matrices linear, generalized linear, Cox proportional hazards outcome models external internal validation study designs.

title: Regression Calibration for Measurement Error Correction
text: functions support single multiple mismeasured exposures.

title: Regression Calibration for Measurement Error Correction
code: # Install devtools if you haven't already install.packages("devtools")  # Install RegCalib from GitHub devtools::install_github("YOUR_USERNAME/RegCalib")

title: Regression Calibration for Measurement Error Correction
text: Suitable linear, GLM, Cox outcome models. Optionally accepts user-supplied uncorrected estimates (e.g., external Cox model). GLM outcome model:
code: library(RegCalib)  result <- RegCalibDF(   ms       = main_data,       # main study data frame   vs       = validation_data, # validation study data frame   sur      = "Z",             # surrogate (mismeasured) variable in main study   exp      = "X",             # true exposure variable in validation study   covCalib = c("V1", "V2"),   # covariates in calibration and outcome model   outcome  = "Y",             # outcome variable   method   = "lm",            # "lm", "glm", or "cox"   external = TRUE             # TRUE = external validation study )  result$correctedCoefTable   # corrected estimates, SE, p-values, 95% CI result$correctedVCOV        # corrected variance-covariance matrix result$standardCoefTable    # uncorrected estimates for comparison result <- RegCalibDF(   ms       = main_data,   vs       = validation_data,   sur      = "Z",   exp      = "X",   covCalib = c("V1", "V2"),   outcome  = "Y",   method   = "glm",   family   = binomial,   link     = "logit",   external = TRUE )

title: Regression Calibration for Measurement Error Correction
text: Suitable linear GLM outcome models. Uses Carroll-Ruppert-Stefanski-Crainiceanu substitution approach analytical sandwich standard errors. internal validation study:
code: result <- RegCalibSub(   ms       = main_data,   vs       = validation_data,   sur      = "Z",   exp      = "X",   covCalib = c("V1", "V2"),   outcome  = "Y",   method   = "lm",   external = TRUE )  result$correctedCoefTable result$correctedVCOV result <- RegCalibSub(   ms          = main_data,      # main study contains both main and validation subjects   sur         = "Z",   exp         = "X",   vsIndicator = "is_validated", # indicator variable: 1 = has validation record   covCalib    = c("V1", "V2"),   outcome     = "Y",   method      = "lm",   external    = FALSE )

title: Regression Calibration for Measurement Error Correction
text: RegCalib imports: stats, dplyr, Matrix, matrixcalc, earth, survival. installed automatically.

title: Regression Calibration for Measurement Error Correction
text: Rosner B, Willett WC, Spiegelman D (1989). Correction logistic relative risk estimates confidence intervals systematic within-person measurement error. Statistics Medicine 8:1051–1069. Rosner B, Spiegelman D, Willett WC (1990). Correction logistic regression relative risk estimates confidence intervals measurement error: case multiple covariates measured error. American Journal Epidemiology 132:734–735. Spiegelman D, McDermott , Rosner B (1997). many uses ‘regression calibration’ method measurement error bias correction nutritional epidemiology. American Journal Clinical Nutrition 65:1179S–1186S. Spiegelman D, Carroll RJ, Kipnis V (2001). Efficient regression calibration logistic regression main study/internal validation study designs imperfect reference instrument. Statistics Medicine 20:139–160. Carroll RJ, Ruppert D, Stefanski LA, Crainiceanu CM (2006). Measurement Error Nonlinear Models, 2nd ed. Chapman & Hall/CRC.

title: Regression Calibration for Measurement Error Correction

title: Regression Calibration Using the Deattenuation Factor Method — RegCalibDF
code: RegCalibDF(   supplyEstimates = FALSE,   ms,   vs,   sur,   exp,   covCalib = NULL,   covOutcome = NULL,   outcome = NA,   event,   time,   method = "lm",   family = NA,   link = NA,   external = TRUE,   pointEstimates = NA,   vcovEstimates = NA )

title: Regression Calibration Using the Deattenuation Factor Method — RegCalibDF
text: supplyEstimates Logical. TRUE, uncorrected estimates supplied user via pointEstimates vcovEstimates, ms optional. Standard regression results returned case. Default FALSE. ms Main study data frame. Must contain variables specified sur, covCalib, covOutcome. Required supplyEstimates = FALSE. vs Internal external validation study data frame. Must contain variables specified exp, sur, covCalib. sur Character vector mismeasured exposure(s)/covariate(s) (surrogates) main study dataset. exp Character vector correctly-measured exposure(s)/covariate(s) validation dataset. Must correspond one--one sur length. covCalib Character vector correctly-measured covariates adjust calibration model outcome model. Default NULL. covOutcome Character vector correctly-measured risk factors outcome associated exposure surrogate. included outcome model must overlap covCalib. Default NULL. outcome Character. Name outcome variable. Required method "lm" "glm". event Character. Name event status indicator Cox models (0 = censored, 1 = event). Required method = "cox". time Character. Name follow-time variable Cox models. Required method = "cox". method Character. Outcome modelling method: "lm", "glm", "cox". Default "lm". family Family function glm (e.g., binomial). character string. Required method = "glm". link Character. Link function glm (e.g., "logit"). Required method = "glm". external Logical. TRUE (default) external validation study; FALSE internal validation study. external = FALSE, vs must contain outcome variable supplyEstimates must FALSE. pointEstimates Named numeric vector uncorrected point estimates standard regression (intercept excluded). Names must match (expanded) covariate names covCalib followed covOutcome. Required supplyEstimates = TRUE. vcovEstimates Named square matrix uncorrected variance-covariance estimates (intercept excluded). Column names must match pointEstimates. Required supplyEstimates = TRUE.

title: Regression Calibration Using the Deattenuation Factor Method — RegCalibDF
text: named list containing: correctedCoefTable Data frame corrected estimates, standard errors, Z-values, p-values, 95\ correctedVCOVVariance-covariance matrix corrected estimates. standardCoefTable(supplyEstimates = FALSE) Results uncorrected standard regression. standardVCOV(supplyEstimates = FALSE) Variance- covariance matrix uncorrected standard regression. calibrationModelCoefTableCalibration model slope estimates. calibrationModelVCOVResidual variance-covariance matrix calibration model. Corrects measurement error continuous exposures (covariates) returns corrected coefficients, standard errors, p-values, variance-covariance matrices. Users may supply uncorrected estimates (e.g., Cox logistic models) instead using built-outcome model. external internal validation study designs supported. Based Rosner, Spiegelman & Willett (1989, 1990) Spiegelman, McDermott & Rosner (1997).    Rosner B, Willett WC, Spiegelman D (1989). Correction logistic relative risk estimates confidence intervals systematic within-person measurement error. Statistics Medicine 8:1051–1069.Rosner B, Spiegelman D, Willett WC (1990). Correction logistic regression relative risk estimates confidence intervals measurement error: case multiple covariates measured error. American Journal Epidemiology 132:734–735.Spiegelman D, McDermott , Rosner B (1997). many uses 'regression calibration' method measurement error bias correction nutritional epidemiology. American Journal Clinical Nutrition 65:1179S–1186S.Spiegelman D, Carroll RJ, Kipnis V (2001). Efficient regression calibration logistic regression main study/internal validation study designs imperfect reference instrument. Statistics Medicine 20:139–160.

title: Regression Calibration Using Substitution Method — RegCalibSub
code: RegCalibSub(   ms,   vs,   sur,   exp,   vsIndicator,   covCalib = NULL,   covOutcome = NULL,   outcome = NA,   method = "lm",   family = NA,   link = NA,   external = TRUE )

title: Regression Calibration Using Substitution Method — RegCalibSub
text: ms Main study data frame. Must contain variables specified sur, outcome, covOutcome (). vs External validation study data frame. Must contain variables specified exp, sur, covCalib (). Required external = TRUE. sur Character vector mismeasured exposure(s)/covariate(s) (surrogates) main study dataset. exp Character vector correctly-measured exposure(s)/covariate(s) validation dataset. Must correspond one--one sur length. vsIndicator Character. Name indicator variable main study dataset identifies subjects validation record (1 = validation record). Required external = FALSE. covCalib Character vector correctly-measured covariates adjust calibration model, including non-linear terms. Default NULL. covOutcome Character vector correctly-measured covariates adjust outcome model (corrected exposure), including non-linear terms. Default NULL. outcome Character. Name outcome variable. Required. method Character. Outcome modelling method: "lm" "glm". Default "lm". family Family function glm (e.g., binomial). character string. Required method = "glm". link Character. Link function glm (e.g., "logit" "log"). Required method = "glm". external Logical. TRUE (default) external validation study; FALSE internal validation study embedded ms.

title: Regression Calibration Using Substitution Method — RegCalibSub
text: named list containing: correctedCoefTable Matrix corrected estimates, standard errors, Z-values, p-values, 95\ correctedVCOVVariance-covariance matrix corrected estimates. Corrects measurement error continuous exposures (covariates) returns corrected coefficients, standard errors, p-values, variance-covariance matrices using Carroll-Ruppert-Stefanski-Crainiceanu (CRS) sandwich variance estimator. Supports linear generalized linear outcome models external internal validation study designs. Standard errors derived analytically via sandwich estimator (bootstrap). Non-linear terms interaction terms pre-computed permanent columns input datasets rather specified formula.    Carroll RJ, Ruppert D, Stefanski LA, Crainiceanu CM (2006). Measurement Error Nonlinear Models, 2nd ed. Chapman & Hall/CRC.
